fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: District of Columbia Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to District of Columbia - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity district of columbia, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to district of columbia, respiratory therapist compact district of columbia, district of columbia respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: DC Board of Respiratory Care
board.parent_dept: DC Department of Health, Health Regulation and Licensing Administration (HRLA)

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in District of Columbia.

steps.title: Meet Education Requirements
steps.description: Complete a respiratory care educational program accredited by the Commission on Accreditation for Respiratory Care (CoARC).

steps.title: Pass the NBRC Exam
steps.description: Successfully complete the National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination.

steps.title: Apply for a License
steps.description: Submit a completed application, official transcripts, and required fees to the DC Board of Respiratory Care.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a District of Columbia respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does District of Columbia have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in District of Columbia. To work in District of Columbia, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to District of Columbia?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in District of Columbia while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in District of Columbia
hero.dek: District of Columbia Respiratory Therapist license requires graduation from an accredited program, passing the NBRC examination, and completing the application process.
